name: Prosperous Unification
description: Through its strife and triumphs, this society has reached every young civilization's ambition: a homeworld with unified goals, and a path open to the stars.

name: origin_primal_calling
effects: ship_damage_against_space_fauna_mult: +15.0%; space_fauna_ship_armor_mult: +15.0%; space_fauna_ship_speed_mult: +10.0%; country_capture_chance_add: +15.0%; gravity_snare_cooldown_mult: -25.0%; country_vivarium_max_capacity_mult: +25.0%

name: Under One Rule
description: Amidst violence and hardship, this civilization was unified by a single leader. Consolidating power through the repression of weaker factions, they have now become a fierce advocate for science and technological advancement. In the aftermath, an uneasy peace has descended. But peace rarely lasts.

name: Broken Rule
description: Amidst political turmoil, and without the Luminary's guidance, the people must make their own way in the galaxy.

name: $origin_legendary_leader$
description: $origin_legendary_leader_desc$

name: $origin_legendary_leader$
description: $origin_legendary_leader_desc$

name: Payback
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin This civilization has been visited by an alien empire who forcibly enlightened and enslaved them. They have repelled their invaders, but will they return?
tooltip: - - - - - One ['concept_advanced_empires', Advanced AI Empire] and several regardless of the Galaxy Settings - One Pre-FTL Civilization: Uplifted with the $origin_broken_shackles$ origin

name: Broken Shackles
description: This civilization began as a mutiny aboard a slaver vessel, and is made up of a diverse group of pre-FTL species.
tooltip: - Species from across the galaxy - - Planetary Modifier - Planetary Feature - - - One ['concept_advanced_empires', Advanced AI Empire] and several Pre-FTL Civilization regardless of the Galaxy Settings - One Pre-FTL Civilization: Will be this empire's species and homeworld - One Pre-FTL Civilization: Uplifted with the $origin_payback$ origin

name: Fear of the Dark
description: A century ago, one of the planets in this empire's home system was destroyed by a freak asteroid. Some believed this was a malicious attack by an alien species. Others brushed off these concerns as mere paranoia. The tension between the two groups grew so great that a newly-terraformed planet was granted to the fearmongers.
tooltip: - ['concept_preftl_civilizations', Pre-FTL Civilization] created by a Separatist Faction, formerly part of your Empire, granting events and benefits

name: Overtuned
description: This civilization uses any and all means of organic enhancement, no matter the risk, no matter the cost. After all, it is better to burn out brightly than fade away into obscurity.
tooltip: - Access to ['concept_overtuned_traits', $origin_overtuned$] Traits - Overtuned Traits: - Can be boosted by the Edict - Can be added and removed through Genetic Modification - ['concept_gene_tailoring', $tech_gene_tailoring$]

name: Knights of the Toxic God
description: Centuries ago, this species' homeworld was visited by a Toxic Entity. The ecological catastrophe that followed ruined half the world, but allowed an early unification of the survivors in their reverence for the Toxic God. An Order of Knights has been created to search for the Toxic God, and although many things have changed, the quest continues.
tooltip: - Homeworld: - - Unique ['concept_pc_habitat', Orbital Habitat]: $t$- ['concept_order_keep', $building_order_keep$] $t$- ['concept_orders_demesne', $district_orders_demesne$] $t$- ['concept_politician', $job_politician$] Jobs replaced by - Situation: - Policies: Knightly Duties - ['concept_knight_commandery', Order's Commanderies] on Subject colonies

name: Progenitor Hive
description: This hive has gained evolutionary advantage through semi-independent leaders. These "Offspring" greatly improve overall proficiency, though they require constant oversight.
tooltip: - ['concept_spawning_pools', Spawning Pools] replaced with ['concept_offspring_nests', Offspring Nest] - ['concept_offspring_holding', Offspring Nest] - If $civic_hive_devouring_swarm$ Civic is NOT selected: Sectors can be released as Subjects - Assigned Leaders: Gain additional passive experience - Offspring Outlook - Can construct Offspring Ships

name: Subterranean
description: This civilization may never know why their ancestors migrated below the surface. Was it necessary to ensure self-preservation, or did the promise of bountiful resources allure them? Whatever the case, they successfully adapted, leading to a more environmentally versatile community.
effects: mining_district_housing_add: +200; planet_orbital_bombardment_damage: -0.75; planet_structures_cost_mult: +10.0%; planet_structures_upkeep_mult: +10.0%; planet_building_build_speed_mult: -10.0%; planet_storm_devastation_mult: -25.0%

name: origin_subterranean_machines
effects: mining_district_housing_add: +200; planet_orbital_bombardment_damage: -0.75; planet_structures_cost_mult: +10.0%; planet_structures_upkeep_mult: +10.0%; planet_building_build_speed_mult: -10.0%; planet_storm_devastation_mult: -25.0%

name: Slingshot to the Stars
description: Once, the skies above this civilization were illuminated by shooting stars; then came a long season of darkness. Taking to the heavens in search of answers, the first offworld explorers discovered a dormant (and long abandoned) $quantum_catapult_1$.
tooltip: - Nearby ruined with the  - Effects: Increased to +50%

name: Teachers of the Shroud
description: WARNING: Blocks all Ascension Paths except for .  Long before their first steps onto the galactic stage, this civilization has been supported and shaped by a Shroudwalker coven.

name: Imperial Fiefdom
description: Our first endeavor beyond our home planet was a painful one, suddenly and brutally subjected to the whims of a foreign galactic power. Now we look towards a future in which we no longer live in the shadow of another empire.
tooltip: - Subject of an  - Can select a ['concept_specialist_subject', Specialist Subject] type at the start of the game

name: Here Be Dragons
description: Since time immemorial, this civilization has shared its star system with a formidable dragon. The mysterious creature's behavior sways between distant benevolence and haughty indifference. How this relationship will evolve as the planet dwellers take to the stars remains to be seen.
tooltip: - Inhabited by a protective Sky Dragon, that will become wrathful if displeased

name: Ocean Paradise
description: Since its birth, this civilization has enjoyed peace in a generous environment with no natural enemies, enabling unhindered growth and opulence.

name: Necrophage
description: This civilization evolved as a parasite, forced to feed off other sapient life forms to sustain itself. As it arrives on the galactic stage, other species will either be brought into the fold, or become its food. For the necrophage, there can be no other way.

name: Remnants
description: This civilization once spanned the void, controlling much of the galaxy. They were eventually defeated and almost destroyed, but after a long period of destitution they are returning to the stars.

name: Shattered Ring
description: This civilization inhabits a Ring World built by unknown forerunners. If it can be understood and fully repaired it will grant them great power.
tooltip: - - : Replaced with Colonizable Ring World Segments that have Blockers decreasing Habitability -

name: Void Dwellers
description: This civilization has made its home in space for as long as it has maintained records - life in a typical planetary environment is anathema to them. They live in several orbital habitats that were only recently united.
tooltip: - - - ['concept_void_dweller', $trait_void_dweller_1$] - ['concept_habitat_preference', $trait_pc_habitat_preference$] - ['concept_habitat_1', $tech_habitat_1$] - ['concept_habitat_2', $tech_habitat_2$] - : Resource-rich systems instead of Colonizable Planets - $ap_voidborn_habitat_effect$

name: Scion
description: A Fallen Empire has meddled in the development of this civilization for thousands of years, guiding them onto a path of their choosing.
tooltip: - ['concept_scion', Scion] of a Overlord - Connected to Overlord's capital via a  - Fallen Empire present in the galaxy regardless of Galaxy settings - Chance of receiving powerful gifts from Overlord

name: On the Shoulders of Giants
description: This civilization has hidden boons in their solar system, placed there in a distant past by a mysterious benefactor.

name: Common Ground
description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Finding strength in their differences, they soon decided to face the future, and whatever it might bring, together.

name: Hegemon
description: This civilization established early contact with their immediate alien neighbors. Gradually, over the span of a century, they masterminded the birth of an interstellar union in which they would have a dominant role.

name: Doomsday
description: WARNING: Challenging Origin This civilization's homeworld is highly unstable, and it is only a matter of time before it explodes. Their only hope is to seek refuge elsewhere before it is too late.

name: Calamitous Birth
description: Not native to their 'homeworld', these Lithoids arrived when a meteorite slammed into the planet and killed off most of the native life.
tooltip: - - - : +4 - Available Colony Ships: Lithoid Meteorites - Planets Colonized with Lithoid Meteorites: - $concept_buried_lithoids$: +2 - - ['concept_experimental_crater', $holding_experimental_crater$] on (Subject) colonies

name: $civic_life_seeded$
description: This civilization's home is a paradise, possibly designed just for them.

name: $civic_post_apocalyptic$
description: Baptized by nuclear fire, this civilization has faced total annihilation - and survived. Devastated yet unbroken, they have rebuilt civilization from the ashes of their old world.

name: Clone Army
description: A species of short-lived and infertile soldier clones exceptionally proficient in matters of war, left masterless and alone to question their engineered purpose and uncertain fate.

name: Resource Consolidation
description: This Machine Intelligence has long-since consolidated all resources in their home system into their capital world, covering it entirely with machinery.
tooltip: - - - - If combined with the $civic_machine_assimilator$ Civic: $t$-  - Waives Technology requirements for the $ap_machine_worlds$ Ascension Perk

name: $civic_mechanists$
description: This civilization has been preoccupied with the idea of metallic automatons since the early Steam Age. Although many said it could not be done, the first true robots left the assembly lines long before even rudimentary space flight was achieved.
tooltip: - ['concept_robots', Robot] Pops: 800 - Mechanical Trait: - Homeworld: ['concept_robot_assembly_plant', Robot Assembly Plant] - - ['concept_powered_exoskeletons', $tech_powered_exoskeletons$] - ['concept_robotic_workers', $tech_robotic_workers$] - ['concept_robomodding', $tech_robomodding$]

name: $civic_syncretic_evolution$
description: A second species forms an integral part of this civilization. They are big, strong, and most of them have the intelligence of a particularly dim-witted child. Ancient wars have culled their species of their most aggressive tendencies, leaving them quite servile.

name: Tree of Life
description: This Hive evolved in a symbiotic relationship with a vast Tree. The Tree grants them many benefits, but its loss would cripple them.
tooltip: - - Colonized Planets: Planetary Feature - Colonies without the Tree of Life Sapling: Planetary Decision - ['concept_tree_of_life_holding', $holding_tree_of_life_sapling$] on Subject colonies

name: Galactic Doorstep
description: This civilization has a dormant Gateway in their home system - potential technological benefit, or a looming menace?

name: Lost Colony
description: This civilization originated as a lost and forgotten colony, separated from its homeworld long ago. The struggling colonists endured many hardships before they could build up the necessary technological and industrial base that would allow for a return to space.
tooltip: - Planetary Modifier - An of the same species is present in the galaxy
